articles.headline: Government Launches Ambitious 'National Digital Infrastructure Mission' for Universal Connectivity by 2030
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government has officially rolled out the 'National Digital Infrastructure Mission' (NDIM) with an aim to provide universal, high-speed internet access across India by 2030.
• NDIM integrates existing initiatives like BharatNet, PM-WANI, and optical fiber networks, while also focusing on developing next-gen data centers and secure digital public infrastructure.
• The mission emphasizes public-private partnerships, skill development for digital literacy, and fostering innovation in edge computing and IoT.
• A dedicated task force has been constituted to oversee implementation, funding mechanisms, and address regulatory challenges.
• This initiative is expected to significantly boost India's digital economy, improve service delivery, and bridge the urban-rural digital divide.

Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.

articles.trivia: India is the world's second-largest internet market by users, but significant disparities in access and speed persist, especially in rural areas.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflationary Pressures; Focus on Growth Revival
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly review, deciding to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive time.
• The decision comes against a backdrop of moderating retail inflation, which has shown a downward trend in recent months, nearing the MPC's target band.
• RBI Governor highlighted the need to balance inflation control with supporting economic growth, citing global uncertainties and domestic demand conditions.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation remains within target while remaining agile to evolving economic data.
• Future policy actions will be guided by the trajectory of food inflation, global commodity prices, and the monsoon's impact on agricultural output.

articles.headline: India-Japan 2+2 Ministerial Dialogue Focuses on Indo-Pacific Security and Economic Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The 2+2 Ministerial Dialogue between India and Japan concluded in New Delhi, bringing together their Foreign and Defence Ministers.
• Discussions primarily centered on enhancing cooperation for a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region amidst evolving geopolitical dynamics.
• Key agreements were reached on expanding defense collaboration, including joint exercises, technology transfer, and logistics support.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to strengthening economic ties, particularly in critical and emerging technologies, supply chain resilience, and infrastructure development.
• The dialogue also covered regional issues, multilateral cooperation (QUAD, G20), and counter-terrorism efforts, underscoring the comprehensive strategic partnership.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds 'Right to be Forgotten' in Landmark Data Privacy Ruling
articles.explainer:
• In a significant judgment, the Supreme Court of India has affirmed the 'right to be forgotten' as an integral facet of the fundamental 'Right to Privacy' under Article 21 of the Constitution.
• The ruling allows individuals to seek removal of their personal information from public platforms, particularly online, if it is no longer relevant, necessary, or causes undue harm.
• The Court laid down guidelines for balancing the right to be forgotten with other fundamental rights, such as freedom of speech and expression and the public's right to know.
• This judgment comes amidst ongoing parliamentary discussions on a new Data Protection Bill, providing crucial judicial guidance for its framework.
• Experts suggest the ruling will have far-reaching implications for digital platforms, data aggregators, and individual control over online presence.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Policy to Boost Electric Vehicle Charging Infrastructure Across India
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Power has released a comprehensive new policy framework aimed at accelerating the development of Electric Vehicle (EV) charging infrastructure nationwide.
• The policy introduces simplified licensing procedures, encourages private sector investment through subsidies and incentives, and mandates charging points in new commercial and residential buildings.
• It sets ambitious targets for public charging stations, particularly along national highways and in major urban centers, to alleviate range anxiety among EV users.
• Emphasis is placed on using renewable energy sources for charging stations and promoting standardized charging protocols for interoperability.
• The move is part of India's broader commitment to reducing carbon emissions and achieving its net-zero targets by promoting sustainable mobility.

articles.headline: ISRO Provides Update on Chandrayaan-4 Mission Preparations; Focus on Lunar Sample Return
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has released an update on the progress of its ambitious Chandrayaan-4 mission, slated for launch in late 2026.
• Chandrayaan-4 aims to be India's first lunar sample return mission, designed to collect pristine regolith samples from the Moon's South Pole and bring them back to Earth for scientific analysis.
• The mission involves a complex architecture comprising an orbiter, a lander, an ascent module, and a re-entry module, requiring advanced robotic capabilities.
• Scientists anticipate the samples from the lunar South Pole could provide crucial insights into the Moon's geological history, the presence of water ice, and the origin of the solar system.
• This mission builds upon the successes of Chandrayaan-1, Chandrayaan-2, and Chandrayaan-3, further solidifying India's position in lunar exploration.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Submits Report on Uniform Civil Code Consultations
articles.explainer:
• A high-level Parliamentary Standing Committee has submitted its comprehensive report on the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) to the Lok Sabha Speaker, following extensive consultations with stakeholders.
• The report is expected to detail the committee's findings on the feasibility, implications, and public opinion regarding the implementation of a UCC in India.
• Consultations involved legal experts, religious leaders, women's rights groups, and various community representatives to gather diverse perspectives.
• While the contents of the report are yet to be made public, it is anticipated to provide recommendations on harmonizing personal laws across different religious communities.
• The UCC remains a sensitive and complex issue, enshrined in Article 44 of the Directive Principles of State Policy, aiming for gender justice and national integration.

articles.headline: India Prepares to Unveil Comprehensive National Cyber Security Strategy 2.0
articles.explainer:
• The Indian government is reportedly in the final stages of unveiling its National Cyber Security Strategy 2.0, aimed at bolstering the nation's digital defenses.
• The new strategy addresses emerging threats from state-sponsored attacks, ransomware, and critical infrastructure vulnerabilities, building on the 2013 policy.
• Key pillars include strengthening cyber resilience across government and private sectors, promoting indigenous cyber security products, and enhancing international cooperation.
• It also focuses on developing a skilled cyber workforce, fostering public awareness, and establishing robust incident response mechanisms.
• The strategy is crucial for protecting India's growing digital economy and ensuring national security in an increasingly interconnected world.

articles.headline: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Storage Shows Promising Results
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Heavy Industries has released an interim report highlighting the significant progress under the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Storage.
• The scheme, aimed at boosting domestic manufacturing of high-capacity batteries, has attracted substantial investments and led to the establishment of several gigafactories.
• India is on track to achieve self-reliance in battery manufacturing, crucial for its electric vehicle ambitions and renewable energy storage needs.
• The report notes a considerable increase in local value addition and job creation within the battery manufacturing ecosystem.
• This success reinforces the government's 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision and positions India as a key player in the global battery supply chain.

articles.headline: India Advocates for Enhanced Climate Finance Mechanisms at G20 Finance Ministers Meeting
articles.explainer:
• India's Finance Minister, Nirmala Sitharaman, addressed the G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors (FMCBG) meeting, strongly advocating for greater climate finance for developing nations.
• She emphasized the need for developed countries to fulfill their USD 100 billion annual commitment and proposed new innovative financing mechanisms to bridge the climate finance gap.
• India highlighted the disproportionate impact of climate change on vulnerable economies and called for a just and equitable transition to a green economy.
• Discussions also focused on multilateral development bank reforms to better address climate challenges and ensure sustainable development goals are met.
• The meeting underscored the urgency of climate action and the need for collective global efforts to mobilize resources for mitigation and adaptation.

articles.headline: Initial Findings of All India Tiger Estimation 2025 Indicate Stable to Growing Tiger Population
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has released initial findings from the All India Tiger Estimation 2025, suggesting a stable to moderately growing tiger population.
• The comprehensive census, conducted every four years, utilized advanced methodologies including camera trapping, M-STRIPES app, and DNA analysis.
• While specific numbers are pending final validation, early indicators point to successful conservation efforts in several tiger reserves and protected areas.
• The report also highlights challenges such as habitat fragmentation, human-wildlife conflict, and poaching, which continue to pose threats.
• This estimation is crucial for informing future conservation strategies and ensuring the long-term survival of India's national animal.

articles.headline: National Quantum Mission Progressing Towards Indigenous Quantum Computing Capabilities

• NQM aims to develop quantum computing hardware, quantum communication systems, quantum sensors, and quantum materials domestically.
• Several research collaborations between academic institutions, industry players, and government labs are underway, focusing on different quantum modalities.
• The mission emphasizes skill development and creating a robust ecosystem for quantum research and innovation, positioning India as a global leader in the field.

articles.headline: India-Bangladesh Joint Rivers Commission Meets to Discuss Teesta and Other Transboundary River Issues
articles.explainer:
• The India-Bangladesh Joint Rivers Commission (JRC) convened for its biennial meeting, focusing on crucial transboundary river management and water sharing issues.
• Discussions prominently featured the Teesta River water sharing agreement, a long-standing point of contention, with both sides expressing commitment to finding an amicable solution.
• Other shared rivers, including the Feni, Kushiyara, and Ganga, were also on the agenda, with progress reported on joint basin management and flood forecasting.
• The meeting underscored the importance of water diplomacy in strengthening bilateral ties and addressing the impacts of climate change on shared water resources.
• Experts from both countries presented new data and potential frameworks for equitable water distribution and sustainable river basin development.

articles.headline: Dedicated Freight Corridor Project Achieves Key Milestones; Boosts Logistics Efficiency
articles.explainer:
• Indian Railways' ambitious Dedicated Freight Corridor (DFC) project has announced the completion of significant sections, marking major milestones in its expansion.
• The Eastern Dedicated Freight Corridor (EDFC) and Western Dedicated Freight Corridor (WDFC) are now largely operational, substantially improving freight movement efficiency.
• The DFCs are designed to decongest existing railway lines, reduce transit times for goods, and lower logistics costs for industries.
• This infrastructure upgrade is crucial for enhancing India's competitiveness in manufacturing and trade, aligning with the 'Gati Shakti' master plan.
• Future phases involve extending the corridors and integrating them with multimodal logistics hubs, further streamlining the supply chain.

articles.headline: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme Bolsters Early-Stage Innovation Across Diverse Sectors

• It has been instrumental in fostering innovation in Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities, promoting inclusive entrepreneurship and job creation.
• The success of SISFS underscores the government's commitment to nurturing a vibrant startup ecosystem and driving economic growth through innovation.

articles.headline: Ministry of Culture Unveils 'Parampara Poshak' Scheme to Revitalize Traditional Indian Arts and Crafts
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture has launched the 'Parampara Poshak' Scheme, a new initiative aimed at preserving, promoting, and revitalizing India's rich heritage of traditional arts and crafts.
• The scheme provides financial assistance, market linkages, and skill development programs for artisans, weavers, and craftspeople, especially those from marginalized communities.
• It focuses on creating digital platforms for showcasing traditional art forms, facilitating direct sales, and connecting artisans with national and international markets.
• Efforts include documentation of endangered art forms, intergenerational transfer of knowledge, and fostering innovation while respecting traditional techniques.

articles.headline: Draft Coastal Zone Management Plan Released for Public Consultation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has released the draft Coastal Zone Management Plan (CZMP) for several coastal states and Union Territories for public consultation.
• The CZMP aims to regulate development activities in coastal areas, protect sensitive ecosystems like mangroves and coral reefs, and ensure sustainable livelihoods for coastal communities.
• The new plans incorporate lessons from past environmental disasters and integrate climate change adaptation strategies, including sea-level rise projections.
• Stakeholders, including fishing communities, environmental groups, and industries, are invited to provide feedback before finalization.
• Effective coastal zone management is critical for balancing economic development with ecological preservation and disaster risk reduction.

articles.headline: India-Pakistan Hold Talks on Indus Water Treaty; Focus on Technical Issues
articles.explainer:
• Officials from India and Pakistan held routine bilateral talks under the framework of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT), focusing primarily on technical aspects of water sharing.
• The Permanent Indus Commission, comprising water commissioners from both nations, reviewed the exchange of hydrological data and discussed concerns regarding certain hydroelectric projects.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to upholding the provisions of the IWT, which has survived several conflicts and remains a cornerstone of water resource management in the region.
• While no major breakthroughs were reported on contentious issues, the continuation of dialogue is seen as a positive step for regional stability.
• The IWT, brokered by the World Bank, governs the sharing of the Indus River system between the two countries.

articles.headline: Government Considers New Policy Framework for Agricultural Biotechnology to Boost Crop Yields
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare is reportedly drafting a new policy framework to streamline regulations for agricultural biotechnology and genetically modified (GM) crops.
• The move aims to leverage advanced biotechnological tools to enhance crop yields, improve nutritional content, and develop climate-resilient varieties.
• The policy seeks to address long-standing concerns regarding regulatory approvals, biosafety assessments, and public acceptance of GM crops in India.
• Emphasis will be placed on robust scientific evaluation, transparent communication, and ensuring farmer benefits and food security.
• This initiative could potentially revolutionize Indian agriculture, but also raises debates around ethical implications and environmental impact.

prelimsMocks.question: The 'Right to be Forgotten' has recently been affirmed by the Supreme Court as an integral part of which fundamental right under the Indian Constitution?
options: (a) Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression (Article 19)
(b) Right to Life and Personal Liberty (Article 21)
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Right to Life and Personal Liberty (Article 21)
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Supreme Court has affirmed the 'right to be forgotten' as an integral facet of the fundamental 'Right to Privacy', which itself is derived from Article 21, the Right to Life and Personal Liberty.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the All India Tiger Estimation (Tiger Census):
1. It is conducted every four years by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
2. The M-STRIPES app is used for data collection in this estimation.
3. India is home to approximately 50% of the world's wild tiger population.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The All India Tiger Estimation is indeed conducted every four years. Statement 2 is correct: M-STRIPES (Monitoring System for Tigers – Intensive Protection and Ecological Status) is an integral part of the data collection methodology. Statement 3 is incorrect: India is home to over 70% of the world's wild tiger population, not 50%.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a primary objective of India's National Quantum Mission (NQM)?

prelimsMocks.explanation: The National Quantum Mission (NQM) aims to develop indigenous quantum computing hardware, quantum communication systems, quantum sensors, and quantum materials. The focus is on fostering an ecosystem and indigenous development.

GS2.headline: Government Launches Ambitious 'National Digital Infrastructure Mission' for Universal Connectivity by 2030
GS2.explainer:

• This initiative is expected to significantly boost India's digital economy, improve service delivery, and bridge the urban-rural digital divide.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds 'Right to be Forgotten' in Landmark Data Privacy Ruling
GS2.explainer:
• In a significant judgment, the Supreme Court of India has affirmed the 'right to be forgotten' as an integral facet of the fundamental 'Right to Privacy' under Article 21 of the Constitution.
• The ruling allows individuals to seek removal of their personal information from public platforms, particularly online, if it is no longer relevant, necessary, or causes undue harm.
• The Court laid down guidelines for balancing the right to be forgotten with other fundamental rights, such as freedom of speech and expression and the public's right to know.
• This judgment comes amidst ongoing parliamentary discussions on a new Data Protection Bill, providing crucial judicial guidance for its framework.
• Experts suggest the ruling will have far-reaching implications for digital platforms, data aggregators, and individual control over online presence.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Submits Report on Uniform Civil Code Consultations
GS2.explainer:
• A high-level Parliamentary Standing Committee has submitted its comprehensive report on the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) to the Lok Sabha Speaker, following extensive consultations with stakeholders.
• The report is expected to detail the committee's findings on the feasibility, implications, and public opinion regarding the implementation of a UCC in India.
• Consultations involved legal experts, religious leaders, women's rights groups, and various community representatives to gather diverse perspectives.
• While the contents of the report are yet to be made public, it is anticipated to provide recommendations on harmonizing personal laws across different religious communities.
• The UCC remains a sensitive and complex issue, enshrined in Article 44 of the Directive Principles of State Policy, aiming for gender justice and national integration.

GS2.headline: India Advocates for Enhanced Climate Finance Mechanisms at G20 Finance Ministers Meeting
GS2.explainer:
• India's Finance Minister, Nirmala Sitharaman, addressed the G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors (FMCBG) meeting, strongly advocating for greater climate finance for developing nations.
• She emphasized the need for developed countries to fulfill their USD 100 billion annual commitment and proposed new innovative financing mechanisms to bridge the climate finance gap.
• India highlighted the disproportionate impact of climate change on vulnerable economies and called for a just and equitable transition to a green economy.
• Discussions also focused on multilateral development bank reforms to better address climate challenges and ensure sustainable development goals are met.
• The meeting underscored the urgency of climate action and the need for collective global efforts to mobilize resources for mitigation and adaptation.

GS2.explainer:
• Officials from India and Pakistan held routine bilateral talks under the framework of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT), focusing primarily on technical aspects of water sharing.
• The Permanent Indus Commission, comprising water commissioners from both nations, reviewed the exchange of hydrological data and discussed concerns regarding certain hydroelectric projects.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to upholding the provisions of the IWT, which has survived several conflicts and remains a cornerstone of water resource management in the region.
• While no major breakthroughs were reported on contentious issues, the continuation of dialogue is seen as a positive step for regional stability.
• The IWT, brokered by the World Bank, governs the sharing of the Indus River system between the two countries.

GS2.trivia: The Indus Water Treaty, signed in 1960, is one of the most successful water-sharing treaties in the world, having survived multiple wars between India and Pakistan.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflationary Pressures; Focus on Growth Revival
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly review, deciding to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive time.
• The decision comes against a backdrop of moderating retail inflation, which has shown a downward trend in recent months, nearing the MPC's target band.
• RBI Governor highlighted the need to balance inflation control with supporting economic growth, citing global uncertainties and domestic demand conditions.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation remains within target while remaining agile to evolving economic data.
• Future policy actions will be guided by the trajectory of food inflation, global commodity prices, and the monsoon's impact on agricultural output.

GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members: three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils New Policy to Boost Electric Vehicle Charging Infrastructure Across India
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Power has released a comprehensive new policy framework aimed at accelerating the development of Electric Vehicle (EV) charging infrastructure nationwide.
• The policy introduces simplified licensing procedures, encourages private sector investment through subsidies and incentives, and mandates charging points in new commercial and residential buildings.
• It sets ambitious targets for public charging stations, particularly along national highways and in major urban centers, to alleviate range anxiety among EV users.
• Emphasis is placed on using renewable energy sources for charging stations and promoting standardized charging protocols for interoperability.
• The move is part of India's broader commitment to reducing carbon emissions and achieving its net-zero targets by promoting sustainable mobility.

GS3.headline: ISRO Provides Update on Chandrayaan-4 Mission Preparations; Focus on Lunar Sample Return
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has released an update on the progress of its ambitious Chandrayaan-4 mission, slated for launch in late 2026.
• Chandrayaan-4 aims to be India's first lunar sample return mission, designed to collect pristine regolith samples from the Moon's South Pole and bring them back to Earth for scientific analysis.
• The mission involves a complex architecture comprising an orbiter, a lander, an ascent module, and a re-entry module, requiring advanced robotic capabilities.
• Scientists anticipate the samples from the lunar South Pole could provide crucial insights into the Moon's geological history, the presence of water ice, and the origin of the solar system.
• This mission builds upon the successes of Chandrayaan-1, Chandrayaan-2, and Chandrayaan-3, further solidifying India's position in lunar exploration.

GS3.headline: India Prepares to Unveil Comprehensive National Cyber Security Strategy 2.0
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian government is reportedly in the final stages of unveiling its National Cyber Security Strategy 2.0, aimed at bolstering the nation's digital defenses.
• The new strategy addresses emerging threats from state-sponsored attacks, ransomware, and critical infrastructure vulnerabilities, building on the 2013 policy.
• Key pillars include strengthening cyber resilience across government and private sectors, promoting indigenous cyber security products, and enhancing international cooperation.
• It also focuses on developing a skilled cyber workforce, fostering public awareness, and establishing robust incident response mechanisms.
• The strategy is crucial for protecting India's growing digital economy and ensuring national security in an increasingly interconnected world.

GS3.headline: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Storage Shows Promising Results
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Heavy Industries has released an interim report highlighting the significant progress under the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Storage.
• The scheme, aimed at boosting domestic manufacturing of high-capacity batteries, has attracted substantial investments and led to the establishment of several gigafactories.
• India is on track to achieve self-reliance in battery manufacturing, crucial for its electric vehicle ambitions and renewable energy storage needs.
• The report notes a considerable increase in local value addition and job creation within the battery manufacturing ecosystem.
• This success reinforces the government's 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision and positions India as a key player in the global battery supply chain.

GS3.headline: Initial Findings of All India Tiger Estimation 2025 Indicate Stable to Growing Tiger Population
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has released initial findings from the All India Tiger Estimation 2025, suggesting a stable to moderately growing tiger population.
• The comprehensive census, conducted every four years, utilized advanced methodologies including camera trapping, M-STRIPES app, and DNA analysis.
• While specific numbers are pending final validation, early indicators point to successful conservation efforts in several tiger reserves and protected areas.
• The report also highlights challenges such as habitat fragmentation, human-wildlife conflict, and poaching, which continue to pose threats.
• This estimation is crucial for informing future conservation strategies and ensuring the long-term survival of India's national animal.

GS3.headline: National Quantum Mission Progressing Towards Indigenous Quantum Computing Capabilities
GS3.explainer:
• The Department of Science & Technology (DST) has provided an update on the progress of India's National Quantum Mission (NQM), launched to foster quantum technology development.
• NQM aims to develop quantum computing hardware, quantum communication systems, quantum sensors, and quantum materials domestically.
• Several research collaborations between academic institutions, industry players, and government labs are underway, focusing on different quantum modalities.
• The mission emphasizes skill development and creating a robust ecosystem for quantum research and innovation, positioning India as a global leader in the field.
• Quantum technologies are expected to revolutionize various sectors, including defense, healthcare, finance, and secure communications.

GS3.headline: Dedicated Freight Corridor Project Achieves Key Milestones; Boosts Logistics Efficiency
GS3.explainer:
• Indian Railways' ambitious Dedicated Freight Corridor (DFC) project has announced the completion of significant sections, marking major milestones in its expansion.
• The Eastern Dedicated Freight Corridor (EDFC) and Western Dedicated Freight Corridor (WDFC) are now largely operational, substantially improving freight movement efficiency.
• The DFCs are designed to decongest existing railway lines, reduce transit times for goods, and lower logistics costs for industries.
• This infrastructure upgrade is crucial for enhancing India's competitiveness in manufacturing and trade, aligning with the 'Gati Shakti' master plan.
• Future phases involve extending the corridors and integrating them with multimodal logistics hubs, further streamlining the supply chain.

GS3.headline: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme Bolsters Early-Stage Innovation Across Diverse Sectors
GS3.explainer:
• The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) has released an impact assessment report on the Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS).
• The report highlights that SISFS has successfully supported over 1,000 early-stage startups across various sectors, including FinTech, AgriTech, HealthTech, and DeepTech.
• The scheme provides financial assistance to startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, and market-entry.
• It has been instrumental in fostering innovation in Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities, promoting inclusive entrepreneurship and job creation.
• The success of SISFS underscores the government's commitment to nurturing a vibrant startup ecosystem and driving economic growth through innovation.

GS3.headline: Draft Coastal Zone Management Plan Released for Public Consultation
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has released the draft Coastal Zone Management Plan (CZMP) for several coastal states and Union Territories for public consultation.
• The CZMP aims to regulate development activities in coastal areas, protect sensitive ecosystems like mangroves and coral reefs, and ensure sustainable livelihoods for coastal communities.
• The new plans incorporate lessons from past environmental disasters and integrate climate change adaptation strategies, including sea-level rise projections.
• Stakeholders, including fishing communities, environmental groups, and industries, are invited to provide feedback before finalization.
• Effective coastal zone management is critical for balancing economic development with ecological preservation and disaster risk reduction.

GS3.headline: Government Considers New Policy Framework for Agricultural Biotechnology to Boost Crop Yields
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare is reportedly drafting a new policy framework to streamline regulations for agricultural biotechnology and genetically modified (GM) crops.
• The move aims to leverage advanced biotechnological tools to enhance crop yields, improve nutritional content, and develop climate-resilient varieties.
• The policy seeks to address long-standing concerns regarding regulatory approvals, biosafety assessments, and public acceptance of GM crops in India.
• Emphasis will be placed on robust scientific evaluation, transparent communication, and ensuring farmer benefits and food security.
• This initiative could potentially revolutionize Indian agriculture, but also raises debates around ethical implications and environmental impact.
